question_text: A ball is thrown upward with an initial velocity of 20 m/s on Earth (g = 10 m/s²). Ignoring air resistance, how long will it take for the ball to return to its starting height, and what will its velocity be when it returns?
correct_answer: Time to return = 2 × (initial velocity ÷ g) = 2 × (20 ÷ 10) = 4 seconds. Velocity when returning = 20 m/s downward (same speed as thrown, but opposite direction). The symmetry occurs because gravitational acceleration is constant throughout the motion.
explanation: The ball takes 2 seconds to reach its highest point (where v = 0) and another 2 seconds to fall back down, totaling 4 seconds. Due to the constant gravitational acceleration, the ball returns with the same speed it left with but in the opposite direction—this is the symmetry of projectile motion.
